title: Scope metadata — relational / cyclical evidence model
description: Every quantifiable claim must define its scope: conditions, cycles, cross-domain rhymes, and relational dependencies. Evidence is a verb, not a noun.

definition.description: Operational or mathematical definition of what is being claimed.

domain.description: Category of invariant (spatial_optimization, energy_efficiency, resonance, etc.).

condition.description: Environmental and boundary conditions under which the pattern holds.

temporal_range.description: Time window, cycle count, or 'timeless' for purely geometric constants.

dependencies.description: Entity IDs or physical constants this claim depends on.

type.description: Nature of the evidence. 'cyclic_observation' means the pattern repeats across cycles. 'cross_domain_rhyme' means it appears in unrelated substrates. 'relational_coherence' means it holds within a web of dependencies.

reproducibility.description: Over how many cycles / seasons / independent observations has this pattern been observed?

cross_domain_count.description: Number of distinct ontologies or substrates where the same pattern appears.

cross_domain_examples.description: Specific entities or systems showing the same pattern.

relational_dependencies_verified.description: Have the linked entities (in 'dependencies') themselves been observed to cohere?

confidence.description: Overall confidence in this evidence. For relational evidence, this should be informed by cycle count, cross-domain breadth, and dependency coherence, not by source prestige.

falsifiability.description: What observation or relational failure would falsify this claim?

measurement_limits.description: Known limits of observation or calculation.

relational_confidence.description: Computed confidence based on cycle recurrence, cross-domain rhyme count, and dependency coherence. This is the engine's trust score, not a human-assigned number.
